research.title: Efficiently Visualizing Large Graphs
research.subtitle: Deans' Undergraduate Research Fund (DURF)
highlights: Proposed SPLEE, a new graph embedding method based on Laplacian eigenmaps and shortest paths.
Proposed t-SGNE, a new dimension reduction method designed for graphs, improving runtime while maintaining visualization quality.
Proposed a new graph visualization method suitable for large graphs by combining SPLEE and t-SGNE.

projects.title: Scikit-learn
projects.subtitle: Open Source Development
projects.remarks: Python, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, pytest, asv

projects.title: Pandas
projects.subtitle: Open Source Development

projects.title: YouTube Interface Customizer
projects.subtitle: Course Project
highlights: Built a Firefox extension that allows users to change color themes and hide, rearrange, or customize various elements of the YouTube interface.
Created the documentations of features and contribution guides, and released (self-distributed) v1.0 at Mozilla Add-ons.

projects.title: Simple SQL Optimizer
projects.subtitle: ByteDance 4th Youth Training Camp
highlights: Implemented an SQL parser that accepts various operators including Filter, GroupBy, Join, etc. to form an abstract syntax tree.
Implemented rule-based predicate pushdown, limit pushdown, and column pruning to optimize the abstract syntax tree.
Implemented cost-based join reordering to optimize the abstract syntax tree.
